title: Compaction Pipeline: SH Reduction, Pruning, and Size Analysis
description:
## Lab 3: Building a Compaction Pipeline

You will build a sequential compaction pipeline on a synthetic 3DGS dataset (50,000 Gaussians with full 59-parameter layout), apply SH degree reduction, opacity-based pruning, and scale-based floater removal, and measure the cumulative size reduction at each step.

### Part 1 — Pipeline Stages

1. **Baseline**: 50K Gaussians, 59 params, float32 — compute exact uncompressed size
2. **SH reduction**: test degree 0, 1, 2, 3 and measure RMSE impact on reconstructed colors
3. **Opacity pruning**: sweep thresholds 0.01, 0.05, 0.1 — plot surviving Gaussians vs. threshold
4. **Scale pruning**: remove large-scale floaters above the 99th percentile of max eigenvalue
5. **Combined pipeline**: apply all three, report compression ratio and discuss trade-offs

### Quantization Preview

As a preview of Module 5, you will also measure the entropy of each attribute category and estimate the theoretical minimum bit-width from the Shannon entropy bound.

---

## Part 2: Design Challenge

In Part 1 you characterized a pipeline by measuring it. In Part 2 you will act as the engineer: choose a deployment scenario, **write your strategy and predictions before running any code**, then implement and evaluate the gap.

### Choose one scenario

**Scenario A — Museum kiosk**: a high-detail scan of a polished marble sculpture, 800K Gaussians. Constraint: ≤ 15 MB at ≥ 28 dB PSNR. The marble surface has strong specular highlights and subtle subsurface color variation.

**Scenario B — Web viewer embed**: a furnished indoor apartment — matte walls, fabric sofas, wooden floors, 1.4M Gaussians. Constraint: ≤ 30 MB at ≥ 26 dB PSNR.

**Scenario C — Mobile AR anchor**: a small outdoor plaza with metal benches and a glass storefront, 2.2M Gaussians. Constraint: ≤ 20 MB at ≥ 24 dB PSNR.

The notebook simulates each scenario with synthetic Gaussian fields whose statistical properties (opacity distribution, SH coefficient variance, scale distribution) match the described scene types.

### Steps

1. **Write a design brief** (before running any code cell in this section): specify which levers you will apply, in what order, the SH degree you will target, the opacity pruning threshold you predict will be safe, and your expected compression ratio and PSNR impact at each stage. Commit this to the notebook as a markdown cell.
2. **Implement your strategy** using the tools from Part 1. Structural methods (ScaffoldGS, HAC) are out of scope here — they require retraining; revisit in Module 5.
3. **Record actual outcomes**: size and color RMSE at each stage vs. your predictions in the design brief.
4. **Interpret the gap**: where were your predictions off? Was the opacity distribution more concentrated than assumed? Did SH reduction hurt quality more than expected for the scene type? Write a short post-mortem (3–5 sentences) explaining the largest discrepancy.
objectives: Implement SH degree truncation and measure its effect on view-dependent color reconstruction.
Implement and sweep opacity-threshold pruning, plotting the retention–threshold curve.
Remove floater Gaussians by thresholding the maximum eigenvalue of the scale matrix.
Build a combined pipeline and report per-stage size reduction.
Compute the empirical entropy of each attribute category and discuss quantization headroom.
Select a deployment scenario and write a compaction strategy brief with quantitative predictions before implementation.
Implement the designed strategy and record per-stage size and quality outcomes against predictions.
Evaluate the gap between predicted and actual results and articulate the sources of prediction error.
